hard.question: What does the explicit keyword do when applied to a constructor in C++?
What is a dangling pointer in C++?
options: A pointer that points to a deallocated memory location
A pointer that points to the beginning of an array
A pointer that points to the end of an array
A pointer that is not initialized

hard.question: What is the output? int x = 5; int y = x > 3 ? x : 3; cout << y;
What is function overloading in C++?
options: Calling a function more times than allowed
Declaring a function with no parameters
Defining multiple functions with the same name but different parameters
Declaring a function with a default parameter

hard.question: What is the purpose of the this pointer in C++?
options: It points to the base class of the current object
It points to the current object instance inside a member function

hard.question: Which of the following best describes polymorphism in C++?
options: The ability to create multiple objects from one class
The ability to inherit properties from a base class
The ability for a single interface to represent different underlying types at runtime

hard.question: What is an abstract class in C++?
options: A class with no member functions
A class that cannot have constructors
A class with at least one pure virtual function, which cannot be instantiated directly
A class that only contains static members

hard.question: What is the output? int a = 10; int b = 20; cout << (a & b);
What is the purpose of the virtual keyword in C++?
options: To indicate that a function can be overridden in derived classes and supports dynamic dispatch
To prevent a function from being overridden
To indicate that a function is static

hard.question: What is the output? int x = 5; int y = 10; cout << (x > y ? x : y);
What is the output? int a = 5; int b = 3; cout << (a / b);
What is the output? int x = 5; int y = 10; cout << (x & y);
